churches.name: Syracuse Reformed Presbyterian Church
churches.notes: Westminster Standards. Covenanter heritage. Exclusive psalmody (a cappella worship). Amillennial. Pastor Jason Housewright. Clerk: Eric Charlton. Address: 2517 South Salina St., Syracuse, NY 13205. Part of the RPCNA St. Lawrence Presbytery. Website: https://www.syracuserpchurch.org. Syracuse Reformed Presbyterian Church was organized in 1849 as a congregation of the Reformed Presbyterian Church of North America by sons and daughters of Scotch-Irish immigrants. Reformed Presbyterians took an early stand against slavery, and the founders were active in the Syracuse abolitionist movement. The bell raised at the end of the Civil War in 1865 still calls the community to worship each Lord's Day. The congregation holds to the Westminster Confession and Catechisms, worships with the unaccompanied singing of biblical psalms, and treasures the Covenanter heritage symbolized by the Blue Banner inscribed For Christ's Crown and Covenant. Government is by elected elders under the upstate New York presbytery.
